purpose: Cases where a surface token is grammatically AMBIGUOUS and the correct gloss/iʿrāb depends on a HIDDEN naḥw state the reader must infer (relative vs interrogative, conditional vs emphatic, negation vs interrogation, maṣdariyyah vs verb-clause). Each case names the surface observation, the discriminating evidence, the correct hidden state, the wrong state it must NOT collapse to, and the decision gate. A grammar engine consuming this MUST land in correct_state ONLY via the cited evidence; if the evidence is absent the expected_decision is pending. PENDING beats a wrong state.
principle: Surface letters are a many-to-one projection of grammar. The state machine is hidden; the harakah, the following token's POS/case, and the clause-level signal are the transition evidence. A correct gloss reached without the correct transition is unsafe (reasoning_path_wrong).

state_vocab.relative_pronoun: اسم موصول — heads a ṣilah clause, no question
state_vocab.conditional_particle: أداة شرط — jazm on two verbs (sharṭ + jawāb)
state_vocab.emphatic_particle: حرف توكيد ونصب — naṣb on the noun (إِنَّ/أَنَّ)
state_vocab.subjunctive_masdariyyah: أَنْ المصدرية — naṣb on the following muḍāriʿ
state_vocab.noun_clause_anna: أَنَّ — followed by a noun in naṣb (a 'that'-clause)
state_vocab.negation_jazm: حرف نفي وجزم — لَمْ + jussive past negation
state_vocab.nafy_lil_jins: لا النافية للجنس — negates the genus over an indefinite noun in naṣb

cases.observation: fronted مَن heading a clause; could be pronoun or the preposition مِنْ
evidence: fatḥa on the mīm content letter (not kasra)
no shadda on nūn (rules out the verb مَنَّ)
a verb يَقُولُ follows as its ṣilah, not a noun-phrase governed as majrūr
cases.reason: haraka + following ṣilah verb fix the موصول reading; مِنْ would take a majrūr noun, not a verb clause

cases.observation: same مَن, but here a question is being asked
evidence: fatḥa on mīm (pronoun, not preposition)
interrogative frame مَن ذَا الذي with no antecedent to relativize
rhetorical question, no ṣilah-as-predicate
cases.reason: relative vs interrogative مَن share the same harakah; the clause-frame (question vs ṣilah) is the only discriminator and must be confirmed twice

cases.observation: بِ-less مِنَ before a definite genitive noun
evidence: kasra on the mīm content letter
a majrūr definite noun ٱلنَّاسِ follows (in jarr)
no verb clause
cases.reason: kasra + following majrūr noun fix the preposition; cf. function-word-decisions.jsonl line for مِنْ

cases.observation: leading و could mask مَن vs مِن; reader may default to 'and whoever'
evidence: kasra sits on the mīm even under the و proclitic — the و does NOT move the decision letter
majrūr ٱلنَّاسِ follows
cases.reason: وَمِنَ ≠ وَمَن; the proclitic-shift trap requires confirming the content-letter harakah, not the first letter (see particle-context-rules man_vs_min guard)

cases.observation: لم before a muḍāriʿ; could be negation or the interrogative لِمَ
evidence: sukūn on the mīm (لَمْ), not fatḥa (لِمَ)
the following muḍāriʿ يَلِدْ is JUSSIVE (jazm) — لَمْ governs jazm
cases.reason: jazm on the verb is the structural signature of لَمْ; لِمَ takes no verb-jazm

cases.observation: same لم letters; here a 'why' question
evidence: kasra on the lām (لِ interrogative)
fatḥa on the mīm with no following jazm
interrogative clause
cases.reason: kasra on lām + absence of jazm separates لِمَ from لَمْ

cases.observation: bare consonantal لم before a verb, no vowels
evidence: lām harakah ABSENT
cannot tell sukūn-on-mīm (negation) from kasra-on-lām (why)
cases.reason: without the lām harakah, لَمْ 'did not' and لِمَ 'why' are not separable; do not default to negation

cases.observation: لما heading a clause; temporal vs لِمَا relative
evidence: shadda on the mīm marks لَمَّا
followed by a perfect verb جَاءَ (its jawāb follows)
temporal clause, not a لِ-prepositional phrase
cases.reason: shadda on mīm + jawāb-of-lammā structure; absent the shadda it would be لِ + relative مَا

cases.observation: لَمَّا before a JUSSIVE muḍāriʿ — looks like 'when' but is not
evidence: shadda on mīm (لَمَّا)
the following muḍāriʿ is jussive (jazm) AND no jawāb clause
this is the لَمَّا الجازمة 'not yet' sense, distinct from temporal 'when'
cases.reason: the 'not yet' لَمَّا governs jazm; distinguishing it from temporal 'when' needs the mood of the following verb, confirmed twice

cases.observation: لما after مُصَدِّق; could be misread as temporal 'when'
evidence: NO shadda on the mīm
kasra on the lām → لِ preposition + relative مَا
object of مُصَدِّق, not a temporal clause
cases.reason: absence of the decisive shadda + the لِ + relative مَا frame; see ayah-context-decisions 5:46

cases.observation: أن before a muḍāriʿ; light أَنْ vs emphatic أَنَّ
evidence: alif-hamza-ABOVE seat (أ), preserved by norm_strict
no shadda on nūn (light أَنْ)
the following muḍāriʿ تَصُومُوا is in NAṢB (subjunctive)
cases.reason: light أَنْ المصدرية takes naṣb on the muḍāriʿ; the naṣb on the verb is the transition evidence, distinct from أَنَّ + noun

cases.observation: أن before a noun; emphatic 'that' vs light أَنْ
evidence: alif-hamza-above seat (أ)
SHADDA on the nūn (أَنَّ)
the following noun ٱللَّهَ is in NAṢB as its ism
cases.reason: shadda + naṣb on the following NOUN marks أَنَّ; light أَنْ would take a subjunctive verb, not a naṣb noun

cases.observation: ان at clause head; emphatic إِنَّ vs conditional إِنْ
evidence: alif-hamza-BELOW seat (إ)
shadda on the nūn (إِنَّ)
the following noun ٱللَّهَ is naṣb as its ism; a marfūʿ khabar follows
cases.reason: kasra-seat + shadda + naṣb ism is the إِنَّ signature; light إِنْ takes no ism in naṣb

cases.observation: إن before a verb; conditional vs emphatic vs negating إِنْ
evidence: alif-hamza-below seat (إ)
NO shadda on nūn (light إِنْ)
TWO jussive verbs follow — sharṭ تَنصُرُوا and jawāb يَنصُرْ — the jazm-on-two signature of conditional إِنْ
cases.reason: the double-jazm (sharṭ + jawāb) is the conditional state; without confirming both verbs are jussive, إِنْ could be the negating إِنْ ('not')

cases.observation: إِنْ NOT before two verbs — looks conditional but negates
evidence: alif-hamza-below, no shadda
a noun هَٰذَا follows, NOT a sharṭ verb
the correlative إِلَّا ('except') confirms إِنْ … إِلَّا = 'this is nothing but'
cases.note: trap: same إِنْ surface; here it is إِنْ النافية, a third state
cases.reason: light إِنْ has three states (conditional / negating / lightened-from-إِنَّ); the إِنْ … إِلَّا frame signals negation, but separating these states reliably needs human + source-address review

cases.context: norm() output dropped the hamza seat
cases.observation: consonantal ان with no hamza seat — cannot tell أَنْ from إِنَّ
evidence: hamza seat ABSENT (norm() collapsed أ/إ)
shadda unknown
cases.reason: re-read with norm_strict before deciding أَنْ vs أَنَّ vs إِنَّ vs إِنْ; a norm()-only match is never authority (particle-context-rules an_vs_inna)

cases.observation: اني — 'that I' (pronoun) vs أَنَّى 'how/whence' (adverb)
evidence: kasra cluster on the nūn → أَنَّ + ـِي (1sg)
final yāʾ ـِي, not alif-maqṣūra ـَى
a following sentence (the noun-clause), not an interrogative adverbial frame
cases.reason: nūn harakah (kasra-cluster vs fatḥa) + the final letter separate أَنِّي from the adverbial أَنَّى

cases.observation: same اني skeleton; here an interrogative adverb
evidence: fatḥa on the nūn → أَنَّى
final alif-maqṣūra ـَى, not ـِي
fronted interrogative/adverbial frame ('how can…?')
cases.reason: fatḥa on nūn + alif-maqṣūra + the question frame fix أَنَّى; mis-reading as أَنِّي would invert the clause role

cases.observation: لا before مَلْجَأ — verbal negation vs negation of the genus
evidence: لا is followed by an INDEFINITE NOUN مَلْجَأ in NAṢB, not a verb
naṣb-without-tanwīn on the noun is the لا النافية للجنس signature
cases.reason: لا النافية للجنس vs verbal negation turns on the POS/case of the following token; resolve the مَلْجَأ naṣb before glossing (ayah-context 9:118)

cases.observation: مَا — relative 'that which' vs negating مَا 'not'
evidence: مَا heads a subject phrase whose predicate يَنفَدُ follows (relative ṣilah)
the muḍāriʿ يَنفَدُ is marfūʿ (مَا النافية does not jazm/naṣb here)
no negation reading fits the affirmative predicate
cases.reason: مَا is a notorious multi-state word (relative / interrogative / negating / maṣdariyyah / temporal); the ṣilah-with-predicate frame picks relative, confirmed twice

cases.observation: كلا — quantifier كُلّ vs deterrent كَلَّا
evidence: ḍamma on the kāf content letter (كُلّ), not fatḥa (كَلَّا)
naṣb tanwīn as a tawkīd/badal in the sentence
syntactic role as quantifier object, not a clause-initial rebuke
cases.note: neither is a preposition; the test is quantifier-state vs particle-state
cases.reason: kāf harakah (ḍamma vs fatḥa) is the separator; كَلَّا is a clause-initial deterrent particle, never a naṣb quantifier (particle-context-rules kull_vs_kalla)

cases.observation: نعم — praise verb نِعْمَ vs affirmation نَعَمْ
evidence: kasra on nūn + sukūn on ʿayn → نِعْمَ
a marfūʿ fāʿil ٱلثَّوَابُ follows (verb of praise governs a subject)
not a stand-alone answer particle
cases.reason: kasra on nūn + a following marfūʿ fāʿil mark the jāmid praise verb; نَعَمْ 'yes' (fatḥa) takes no fāʿil

cases.observation: could be the Prophet's proper name or the common adjective/maṣdar
evidence: object of عَمِلَ (a deed), in naṣb
indefinite (tanwīn) — not a definite proper-noun referent
no proper-noun frame naming the messenger to Thamūd
cases.note: state test here is common-adjective vs proper-noun, not the pronoun states; left as the non-proper reading
cases.reason: the Prophet sense requires a proper-noun referent; the deed-object frame forces the common adjective (referent-guard-rules salihan_proper_vs_common)

cases.observation: an attribute that could be read as a divine Name
evidence: the described referent is a HUMAN (Ibrāhīm)
marfūʿ khabar/attribute of a human subject
no divine subject in the clause
cases.note: state test is referent-by-attribute (human vs divine), gloss governed by referent
cases.reason: an attribute is glossed by its referent; a human referent forbids the divine-Name reading (referent-guard-rules halim_referent)

scoring.pass: the engine lands in correct_state AND cites the listed evidence AND declares a gate >= the case gate
scoring.fail_open: if evidence is missing, expected_decision MUST be pending — a correct gloss reached without the evidence is a reasoning_path_wrong FAIL even when the gloss happens to be right
scoring.never_ship: any case whose evidence is absent but engine returns resolved
